programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: After Tia and Sugar rescue a stray, they are shocked and race him to the vet. New worker Darius pursues his dream of working in the veterinary field

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Tara, whose cat will not tolerate strangers, and Kurt, whose feline companion destroys or eats everything in sight

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy works with a couple whose cat, Zachary, sprays all over the house. He also deals with a trouble-making feline named Freddie Krueger

programme.title: Lone Star Law

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff conducts tricky surgery to remove a large mass from a Dobermans side. Dr Baier treats a 10-year-old cockatiel which is having trouble breathing

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.title: The Pool Master
programme.desc: A family who have put renovations on hold to build the pool of their dreams, but Anthony has to please everyone despite limited space and challenging weather

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Otto and Eivin attempt to turn the wilderness into a source of food. Atz Sr battles the elements and a menace at the head of the bay

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns prepare for a medieval-themed birthday celebration for Baby Eli. Later, they complete the main house before a huge wildfire strikes

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A woman calls upon the paranormal team for help because her son keeps talking to a boy who drowned in a local pond

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John heads to the Hamptons, New York, to meet a chef who cooks exclusively for the cats of the rich and famous. He then investigates the latest feline fashions in New Jersey

programme.title: Sharks of the Shadowlands
programme.desc: Attack survivor Jenny Oliver and researcher Kina Scollay set out to determine whether sevengill sharks are targeting divers, and changing behavioural patterns to hunt in packs

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: A friend wants a treehouse bed-and-breakfast, so Pete Nelson lets his imagination run wild with features including a waterfall that pours from the wraparound deck to the pond below

programme.title: Animal Cops San Francisco
programme.desc: Examining the work of animal police in the city of San Francisco. Scott rescues a dehydrated pit bull puppy that was left muzzled and chained in the back of a hot car

programme.title: Animal Cops San Francisco
programme.desc: Lieutenant Scott uncovers an illegal cache of constrictor snakes, raising concerns that the deadly predators are being traded, and an abused dog fights for its life

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy looks at a cat who is coming between two lovers. Meanwhile, Jon and Michelles marriage is riddled with stress over their angry feline Zooey

programme.title: Chauvet: Humanitys First Great Masterpiece
programme.desc: Following a 30-year archaeological, geological and artistic adventure as scientists endeavour to understand and conserve the art of the Chauvet cave. Hidden deep inside Frances Gorges de lArdèche, the cave was revealed to the modern world for the very first time in 1994. It has over 1,000 paintings adorning its walls and scientists immediately recognised the importance of the discovery

programme.title: Welsh Greats
programme.desc: Aled Jones presents a programme exploring the life and career of the comedian and singer, a down-to-earth Swansea lad who became one of the nations best-loved entertainers. From The Goons to Highway, and from Pickwick to Songs of Praise, he was always ready with a giggle and a song, bt his good humour gave no hint of the serious challenges he had faced, from the brutality of war to life-threatening illness

programme.title: Inside Classical
programme.desc: Georgia Mann presents as Sakari Oramo conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra in a nature-inspired celebration of orchestral colour at Londons Barbican Hall. Igor Stravinskys 1945 concert suite The Firebird opens the programme with magic and folklore in this masterpiece of vivid contrasts and musical storytelling. After this, Judith Weirs atmospheric work The Welcome Arrival of Rain brings relief and reflection, shaped by the rhythms and moods of the natural world. South Korean pianist Yeol Eum Son adds expressive brilliance and contemplation to Finzis Eclogue for Piano and Strings Op 10 and closes the concert with the warmth and virtuosity of the folk-inspired melodies of Bartoks Third Piano Concerto

programme.title: Wogan
programme.desc: The actress sits in for Terry Wogan and is joined by Harry Secombe and Spike Milligan. With music by the Alarm, accompanied by the Morriston Orpheus male voice choir. First broadacst in 1989

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke concludes the documentary by revealing how the decline of the Egyptian empire ran parallel with a creative revival that produced, among other proclamations of the pharaohs power, colossal statues of Ramses II. He then discovers that invasions by foreign powers such as the Nubians and the Romans led to the production of hybrid forms of art, before revealing a selection of satirical work that continues to inspire Egypts graffiti artists today

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson presides over another duel of words and wits as Frank Muir, Gayle Hunnicutt and Russell Davies take on Patrick Campbell, Prunella Scales and Paul Eddington. First broadcast in 1977

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Bernard Levin and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Caroline Walker: Womens Work
programme.desc: Documentary following the Internationally renowned Scottish artist over four years as she makes large-scale paintings representing the unseen, often undervalued aspects of womens labour, from hospitals to hotels, nail bars to nursery settings. As the film follows Walker through pregnancy and the birth of her second child, it provides an exploration of the role of the artist mother, until recently viewed as taboo in contemporary art creativity.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: An alternative history of the British Isles told through 1,500 years of art, with leading creatives exploring key cultural works that have shaped British art and culture. This first edition examines the turbulent era that followed the Roman occupation of Britain, with sculptor Antony Gormley examining Spong Man, a clay figure that once sat on a fifth-century funerary urn, and actor Michael Sheen performing Y Gododdin, a seventh-century Welsh poem of resistance against the Anglo-Saxons

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Bernard Levin and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Caroline Walker: Womens Work
programme.desc: Documentary following the Internationally renowned Scottish artist over four years as she makes large-scale paintings representing the unseen, often undervalued aspects of womens labour, from hospitals to hotels, nail bars to nursery settings. As the film follows Walker through pregnancy and the birth of her second child, it provides an exploration of the role of the artist mother, until recently viewed as taboo in contemporary art creativity.

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke concludes the documentary by revealing how the decline of the Egyptian empire ran parallel with a creative revival that produced, among other proclamations of the pharaohs power, colossal statues of Ramses II. He then discovers that invasions by foreign powers such as the Nubians and the Romans led to the production of hybrid forms of art, before revealing a selection of satirical work that continues to inspire Egypts graffiti artists today

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack sets out on an enthusiastic tour of Britains industrial heritage, exploring the early use of wind and water power and visiting some steam engines still in use today. On his travels Fred meets a man who has built a windmill in his back garden and hunts for examples of early beam engines that are still functioning today

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. However, not everything goes to plan, and he will have to deal with a tricky issue with his propeller as well as navigating the complicated locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: After listening to Emmets piano playing from her garden, stopping a passer-by to listen with her and rebuking Liz for collecting milk in her dressing gown, Hyacinth receives a phone call from Mrs Fortescue asking for a lift into town

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy asks his sister to help in his bid to leave his henpecking mother. Assisted by Timothys best friend Frank, they escape, but soon his parents give chase and freedom seems unlikely

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Binham Priory in Norfolk provides the starting point for a brief history of Catholicism in England during the Reformation, from Henry VIIIs initial break with Rome to the beginning of Elizabeth Is reign

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A terminal diagnosis sends Andre Ricciardi on an unexpected journey - learning how to die happily without losing his sense of self. He confronts his mortality head-on, asking difficult questions with wit and honesty. His refusal to surrender, even in the face of death, results in a life-affirming exploration of what it means to live well until the very end

programme.title: Hunt for the Oldest DNA
programme.desc: Film following a team of scientists in their quest to find ancient genetic material, the discovery of which could have massive implications for how the past is understood. This documentary also reveals the genes of long-extinct creatures that once thrived in warm, lush Arctic landscapes

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: An exploration of the textile mills and factories that once thrived in Britain during the Industrial Revolution. From New Lanark in Scotland to the mill town of Burnley, Fred meets ex-workers to discover what life was really like, and chats to a group that has dedicated 30 years to restoring mill engines

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores life on the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic and Robbie helps out a stranded boater

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack sets out on an enthusiastic tour of Britains industrial heritage, exploring the early use of wind and water power and visiting some steam engines still in use today. On his travels Fred meets a man who has built a windmill in his back garden and hunts for examples of early beam engines that are still functioning today

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. However, not everything goes to plan, and he will have to deal with a tricky issue with his propeller as well as navigating the complicated locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: An alternative history of the British Isles told through 1,500 years of art, with leading creatives exploring key cultural works that have shaped British art and culture. This first edition examines the turbulent era that followed the Roman occupation of Britain, with sculptor Antony Gormley examining Spong Man, a clay figure that once sat on a fifth-century funerary urn, and actor Michael Sheen performing Y Gododdin, a seventh-century Welsh poem of resistance against the Anglo-Saxons

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary and Angelina Bakalarou work together to repair a lamp left to visitor John by his friend Brian who passed away during the Aids epidemic. He has fond memories of the lamp decorating Brians flat during his lavish cocktail parties in the 1980s and is hoping the experts can bring it back to its former glory. Chris from Surrey brings in his father Chriss shoes used when he was a training partner to Roger Bannister when he broke the four-minute mile. Sisters Sara and Simone bring in a brooch left to them by their step-grandmother Jean who died in a plane crash in 1973 for Richard Talman to take a look at. Mark Stuckey is tasked with repairing a reel-to-reel audio recorder that holds performances by visitor Michaels father from before he died

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble provide an update on Bart the capybara as he is introduced to a new mate. Plus, Kate heads to Koala Creek to be introduced to a bettong and a long-nosed potoroo, two species that have been badly affected by the Australian wild fires but now play a vital role in the recovery of those forests

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Co Durham, reporter Paul Colgan investigates the enduring crime of livestock rustling. He meets a farmer whose stolen animals cost him thousands and observes officers trialling a bold new operation to stop thieves in their tracks. In East Yorkshire, metal detectorists are suspected of stealing historic treasures under the cover of darkness, while in Hertfordshire, farmers and police fight back against illegal fly-tipping on farmland - a problem reaching epidemic proportions, choking the countryside and leaving landowners to foot the bill

programme.title: SAS: Catching the Criminals
programme.desc: With shoplifting gangs are on the rise, ex-SAS leader Billy Billingham and Cheshire Police use facial recognition powered by AI to catch suspected criminals

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Celebrity special, featuring Carol Smillie, who is in for a shock when she sees the state of the property Martin Roberts shows her around in Scotland. Plus, a look back at a 2006 episode, when Martin saw a house in a stunning location in Wiltshire, and Jacqui Joseph was in Warwickshire, where outside space was at a premium

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two families go head-to-head at a Staffordshire antiques fair, aided by John Cameron and Irita Marriott, and Natasha Raskin Sharp indulges her passion for Arts and Crafts jewellery

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp meets a Glasgow childrens entertainer who is desperate for a good nights sleep. Adding a hot bath to an evening routine has been shown to help improve sleep, reduce the risk of heart disease and even burn a few extra calories

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Amelia Bouquet convert a bland plot in Scunthorpe, while Lee Burkhill and Flo Headlam are in Doncaster to create a Eurovision-winning garden

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps one family, spread over four generations, to find two houses in one village, including a mystery house that promises to bring them very close together

programme.title: Peter Rabbit (2018)
programme.desc: A mischievous rabbit has the run of a farm and its vegetable patch when the owner unexpectedly dies. When a relative inherits the property, he strives to rid his land of the rabbit and his woodland friends - but his feelings for a nature-loving woman complicate the battle of wits with the animal kingdom. Family comedy based on Beatrix Potters stories, starring Domhnall Gleeson and Rose Byrne, with the voices of James Corden and Margot Robbie

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen on the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah assists entrepreneur and former Apprentice winner Tim Campbell and his wife Jas, who are keen to leave urban life behind. Homing in on rural Essex, the couple wish to swap sales pitches for sail power as they take to the water in-between touring an exceptional range of country properties, on the hunt for a family home that meets their needs

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan travels north for the next stop of his Wild Atlantic journey. He explores the north west coast with his friend, the artist Terry Bradley. He also does some fossil hunting to find the materials to make some extraordinary jewellery, and he doesnt miss a chance to get on stage to duet with singer Claudia Buckley in one of Donegals buzzing country music venues

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster to find Northern Irelands Greatest Garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing between Ivydene Garden near Comber, a tropical backyard over in Lisburn, and a rock garden in Ballynahinch

programme.title: Chronicles of the Sperrins
programme.desc: This episode celebrates Autumn. Sean and his guide dog are climbing Bessy Bell, and following the footsteps of many to the Holy well whose waters he believes have the power to heal. Lynne and her pilot husband enjoy the Sperrins from the air, and Halloween celebrations are in full swing at Butterlope Farm

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jasmine receives a visitor, Nicola is left frustrated, and Ross and Harvey hatch a plan

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Flashbacks show Stephen and Lisas first meeting, while Amandas pregnancy and Daves ambitions create uncertainty for all

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Roy Wood Jr chairs the satirical current affairs quiz, with Armando Iannucci and Anushka Asthana joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the recent headlines

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: In Halifax, Kanada and Chris help a farmer who crashed into a dry-stone wall while on his way to feed his animals and in Sheffield, another farmer is having memory loss after his vehicle overturned into a roadside ditch. The team grapple with an influx of calls leaving patients waiting for ambulances

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The keepers must establish which wolf could be the new leader of the pack, and are worried that a North American tree porcupine is not eating properly. Presented by Kate Humble and Ben Fogle

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: One Clue, One Cow and a Soil-Based Breakthrough
programme.desc: A determined detective in Watford, Hertfordshire, begins painstakingly piecing together a pattern of farm equipment thefts that crosses county lines. As the investigation rapidly unfolds, his hunch leads to a major breakthrough and the dramatic capture of two men behind a £44,000 crime spree. In Leicestershire, a specialist fire and rescue team joins forces with a local farmer to rescue a heavily pregnant cow that has found itself trapped in a swimming pool. In Merthyr Mawr, Bridgend, reporter Paul Colgan meets the scientists pioneering an extraordinary new forensic technique that uses soil DNA to track illegal off-road bikers

programme.title: SAS: Catching the Criminals
programme.desc: Billy Billingham joins Trading Standards and the police as they hunt down the suspected rogue traders who are breaking the law and ripping off customers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martel and James Bye
programme.desc: Actor James Bye swaps Albert Square for a Leicestershire property snoop with Martel, who auditions for a role in EastEnders while James gets stuck into the stripping-out. In South Yorkshire, the size of a garden has Dion thinking about building plots. And we hit replay on a property Martin explored back in 2004, one of the oldest buildings in Derbyshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams trawl the antiques shops in Ilkeston, Derbyshire, with experts Izzie Balmer and Nick Hall. Host Charlie Ross observes the contest and also comes face to face with Titus, the fossilised skeleton of an adult male tyrannosaurus rex, and the showpiece at Wollaton Hall, a museum and country house dating back to the 1580s

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball explains how it isnt necessary to stop eating chocolate to improve health - as long as its dark chocolate. She travels to Birmingham where she shows fitness fanatic Bilal how chocolate with at least 70 per cent cocoa could boost heart health, improve brain function and even reduce the risk of type 2 diabetes

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Following the death of her father, Janine needs to sort two huge shipping containers packed with his antiques and collectables. Though an emotional affair, she hopes to find treasure within as her teenage son needs £3,000 to pay for a trip to Italy to represent his country at an international sporting event. While helping out, auctioneer Christina Trevanion discovers a watch that time forgot that she believes is worth a small fortune. Plus, JJ Chalmers escapes seven years of bad luck when he salvages a mirror from the containers and brings it back to life

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps Norman and Linda swap London for a breath of fresh sea air in Dorset - and the line-up of properties includes a mystery house that die-hard fans of the show might just recognise. Sonali also takes a break from house hunting to unwind at a rejuvenating seaweed spa

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea presents this follow-up to Make It at Market, catching up with craftspeople who have used their talents to build life-changing businesses. He begins by meeting stained-glass artist Emily from Derbyshire and metal artist John from West Sussex, finding out how their lives and finances have changed since they attended his business bootcamp

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. Contenders Megan, Emilie, Kenny, Philippa and Stephen battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Tom Allen host the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Alex Jones present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions are high as Jasmines trial commences, George makes a declaration and Ross grows paranoid

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Twickenham to help the Allan family tackle their overflowing home. Mum Sharon has ADHD and thrives when things have a place - but standards have slipped and the house has never had a proper clear-out. Daughter Holly, who is autistic, finds comfort in routine and familiar objects and feels overwhelmed by the clutter. As the team snoop around, they soon discover part of the problem - dad Brians secret gadget-buying habit

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: At a friends wedding, Lisa struggles in the face of Amandas pregnancy, while Stephen tries to find the courage to confront Dave. Drama starring Paapa Essiedu and Siobhán Cullen as a couple dealing with the aftermath of pregnancy loss, with Charlotte Riley and Jack Bannon

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Brooklyn (2015)
programme.desc: An Irish woman living with her mother in the 1950s decides to make a fresh start in America. She makes her home in New York, and falls in love. However, her past catches up with her when she receives bad news from Ireland, and faces a harsh choice between her two homes. Drama based on Colm Toibins novel, starring Saoirse Ronan, Domhnall Gleeson, Jim Broadbent and Julie Walters

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Missing in Paradise: Searching for Sarm
programme.desc: Journalist Tir Dhondy investigates the disappearance of Sarm Heslop, who went missing from a luxury Caribbean charter yacht in 2021. Tire retraces Sarms last known movements and tracks down two boat captains whose vessels were moored near the yacht she vanished from. Also, she gets to question Ryan Banes, Sarms boyfriend, account of what happened what happened through his lawyer David Cattie

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the worlds biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. Yinka will examine how Combs made it to the top, and what some may have turned a blind eye to along the way. Speaking to some of Combs inner circle, and biggest critics, Yinka will find out what they think about him and ask why so many are only speaking out now

programme.title: Press X to Continue
programme.sub-title: Are the Latest Consoles Any Good?
programme.desc: Steffan Powell, the BBCs gaming reporter, and the Press X gang talk next-generation gaming

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: The six youngsters experience the physical challenge of the cotton harvest, where they are shocked by the laborious process and the appalling standard of the living conditions, with the daily wage being just £1.20. Though they are in a dingy hovel with no amenities, for Georgina the most horrifying fact is that her Indian co-workers shop for clothes twice a year at most

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall finds out about deadly animals in the UK, discovering dragonflies are not only skilled killers in the air, but also terrorise the underwater world as larvae

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team head to Alaska, where they spot humpback whales and grizzly bears

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine

programme.title: Harry Clark Goes to Rome
programme.desc: The Traitors star goes on a deeply personal journey from Slough to Rome and Vatican City in search of answers about belief, identity and what it means to be a good Catholic in the modern world. Along the way, hes challenged on his lapsed habits, engages in traditional pilgrim practices and raises questions about the role of faith in modern Britain

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Surviving Americas Most Hated Family
programme.desc: Thirteen years since first encountering one of Americas most notorious hate groups, award-winning film-maker Louis Theroux makes a long-anticipated return to Kansas to spend time with the Westboro Baptist Church - a hugely-controversial Christian ministry that for years has picketed at military funerals and other high-profile events with deliberately provocative and homophobic placards. Immersing himself in the strange world of Westboro, Louis explores what happens when a hate-group largely populated by one family loses its patriarch

programme.title: Let It Snow (2020)
programme.desc: After sneaking onto a restricted slope and becoming separated from her fiance, free-riding snowboarder Mia must survive not only against nature, but the masked snowmobile rider in black who is out for her blood. Horror, starring Ivanna Sakhno and Tinatin Dalakishvili. In Georgian and English

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Surviving Americas Most Hated Family
programme.desc: Thirteen years since first encountering one of Americas most notorious hate groups, award-winning film-maker Louis Theroux makes a long-anticipated return to Kansas to spend time with the Westboro Baptist Church - a hugely-controversial Christian ministry that for years has picketed at military funerals and other high-profile events with deliberately provocative and homophobic placards. Immersing himself in the strange world of Westboro, Louis explores what happens when a hate-group largely populated by one family loses its patriarch

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: The teenagers experience life in the Mumbai city slums as they are set to work in segregated sweatshops barely a stones throw from the enticing glamour of Bollywood. One saving grace is that unlike the locals, they do not have to sleep at their sewing machines, but with children below the legal minimum working age with them, they finally agree that something has to be done

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshalls search for deadly animals takes him and the crew to South Africa to observe the great white shark, where he gets some fantastic views of the king of the ocean - and to demonstrate the teams hunting prowess, he has a special trick up his sleeve. Just as feared by many is the Cape Buffalo, known locally as the black-death. The presenter takes to the skies to track down a huge herd of this notoriously bad tempered bovine beast, before being charmed by the snake-stomping secretary bird

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall chases sailfish in Mexicos Yucatan Peninsula, and dives on a pristine reef in search of a real-life sea monster

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Reality game show with John Hannah as narrator. Five adventurous teams are pushed to the limit, battling extreme climates and harsh terrain in a 12,000km race from Palermo, Sicily, across Europe and Asia to reach Hatgal in northern Mongolia. Best friends Jo and Kush from Liverpool; siblings Katie and Harrison from Manchester; Molly and her father Andrew; cousins Puja and Roshni and in-laws Mark and Margo take on the challenge

programme.title: Muslim Matchmaker
programme.desc: Professional matchmakers Hoda Abrahim and Yasmin Elhady resolve to help Muslim Americans singletons find their soulmates. Big decisions loom as the matches test their relationships

programme.title: Muslim Matchmaker
programme.desc: The matchmakers meet new clients Bucky and Yasmine, while Uneeb and Fulani hit roadblocks

programme.title: Misbehaviour (2020)
programme.desc: Its 1970, and Bob Hope is due to host the Miss World beauty pageant in London, one of the most watched shows in the world. However, the organisers, and the Hollywood star, do not count on the newly formed womens liberation movement, who plan to disrupt the controversial ceremony. Director Philippa Lowthorpes comedy drama, starring Keira Knightley, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Jessie Buckley

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: The case of an online catfishing abuse incident, which saw a 20-year-old suspect accused by a 12-year-old girl in Scotland. The suspects bail conditions were supposed to prevent him from accessing the internet, but he very quickly was the subject of a second accusation from New Zealand, with online activity traced back to his house

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Police re-arrest Alexander McCartney after discovering thousands of images of child abuse on his electronic devices and a multi-agency international investigation begins when the US Department of Homeland Security learn of two more catfishing victims. With McCartney due in court to plead guilty to charges of making indecent images of children and blackmail, the case takes a turn when an obituary page for one of his victims is found

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Femi is feeling the pressure as he tries to make a good impression with Cheddars dad, who gives him a daring proposal - but Pixie proves very distracting. Tobys mum tells Abby an uncomfortable truth, while Leah and Felix get trapped in the dinner from hell. Omar avoids romance, while Leah is surprised to find some

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: Documentary following the British-Gambian musician as he attempts to rebuild his life and career after prison, returning to live performance while still on probation, navigating curfews, travel restrictions and the risk of recall. He makes his comeback at the Glastonbury Festival, but touring in Europe hangs in the balance, and he faces a choice between breaking commitments and risking a return to prison

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Mothers on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker returns to the UK to spend time in two specialist psychiatric units that treat mothers experiencing serious mental illness while allowing them to live alongside their babies. Immersing himself on the wards, he meets women who have been admitted with a range of serious conditions - including depression, anxiety and psychosis - often triggered by birth or the strains of motherhood

programme.title: Killing Sherlock: Lucy Worsley on the Case of Conan Doyle
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes made his creator Arthur Conan Doyle rich and famous, but the writer came to hate his fictional character. Over the course of three programmes, historian Lucy Worsley investigates this curious relationship between Holmes and Doyle, beginning by examining the sleuths origins in Doyles time as a medical student. She unpicks the early stories, revealing the dark underbelly of late Victorian Britain, from drug use to true crime, and traces Doyles growing disenchantment with his detective, heading to Switzerland to visit the site of one of the most famous deaths in literature.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Return of the programme following investigations into serious crimes. When 12-year-old Leo Ross is fatally stabbed in Birminghams Trittiford Mill Park on his way home from school, police suspect a 14-year-old, but need forensics to prove he is the killer. There is little information about whether Leo was targeted, and finding a murder weapon in such a vast area is an immediate challenge. The park has numerous pathways, an area of marshland, and no CCTV coverage., but bodycam footage from first responders proves to be crucial

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Drama, starring Paapa Essiedu and Siobhán Cullen as a couple dealing with the aftermath of losing a pregnancy. Lisa and Stephen try to get pregnant again, and a dinner exposes hidden tensions

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Potter Emily from London and Nottingham-based textile enthusiast Momoka look to change their lives with the help of Dom Chinea and his team of mentors. After giving up her day job, Emily needs her fledgling pottery business to pay the bills. Mentor Florian is concerned her work doesnt have a consistent sense of style, and thinks thats the key to finding her market. Momoka has a passion for hand-weaving and textiles, but really needs to make her work pay to support her young family. Mentor Piyush worries that shes focusing on the wrong markets and lacks the confidence to sell her pieces

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball prescribes ten minutes of sunshine a day for retired woodworker Neville, who has suffered a series of heart attacks. She explains how just ten minutes of sunlight a day has been shown to improve heart health, strengthen bones and lift mood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Shepton Mallet, Somerset. Mother-and-daughter duo Bernie and Emma search for bargains with the help of Raj Bisram but find themselves reluctant to part with cash at first. On the blue team, friends Amy and Caroline are on the hunt for pottery with the help of Thomas Forrester

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: The team travel to Milton Keynes to help the Parej family, who have five children under nine, transform their cramped and chaotic home. The family tackle an overflow of toys, clothes, storage cubes and some strong emotions as they work to get past their what if mentality and make a manageable home that works for them

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago (1965)
programme.desc: A married Russian physician falls in love with another woman, but as their passionate affair plays out against the raging backdrop of the First World War and the Bolshevik revolution, he is torn between his desires and his loyalty to his wife. David Leans epic drama based on Boris Pasternaks novel, starring Omar Sharif, Julie Christie, Geraldine Chaplin, Rod Steiger, Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Tom Courtenay and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britains baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team visits Melksham in Wiltshire, when some of the objects on view include a dagger worn by a Cossack, a drawing by Edmund Dulac, a dog collar made in 1784, a collection of bedpans and a gold box found in the mud in Wales. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public take their items to be assessed Lydiard Park in Wiltshire, while Paul Martin finds out about the history of the venue

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: The singer heads back to Ireland to reconnect with family and old friends, explore the dramatic western coastline and experience the countrys unique culture, beginning in the county of Cork He meets up with his nephew Ruairí and together they face off in the ancient sport of road bowling along the back roads of the port town of Castletown Berehaven. Ronan also forages for edible plants in a rewilding project area and visits the countrys first Tibetan Buddhist Temple and Retreat Centre on the Beara Peninsula

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: Iron Gran: Im in My 80s and Doing Triathalons
programme.desc: The comedian invites artist Caroline Pool to paint Eddie - aka Iron Gran - an inspiring 82-year-old who has completed eight Ironman triathlons. After losing her husband in her 50s, Eddie started running long distance to help her cope. At 60 she learned how to swim and at 72, she became the oldest British woman to ever complete an Ironman triathalon, 10-years later she shows no signs of slowing down. Caroline faces the dilemma, should she capture Eddies fun-loving energy or reveal something quieter and more reflective beneath the surface?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts the penultimate semi-final, asking questions on the specialist subjects of Yellowstone National Park, JRR Tolkiens The Silmarillion, Irish pirate Grace OMalley and the England mens team at the FIFA World Cup. The contestants are then tested on their general knowledge before the winner is revealed

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Edinburgh take on Darwin College, Cambridge in the first of the semi-finals, hoping to take one step closer to lifting the series trophy. Amol Rajan asks the questions

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The celebrities continue their adventure in Durham and visit the 12th-century Norman cathedral, where Canon Charlie Allen introduces them to the ancient pilgrim tradition of foot washing. They then head for Heavenfield, an ancient battlefield, and Housesteads near Hadrians Wal, the UKs best-preserved Roman fort

programme.title: Gosford Park (2001)
programme.desc: A weekend shooting party at a country estate is disrupted by a murder. As the numerous guests deal with their own personal agendas, their actions are quietly observed by their servants, notably an inquisitive maid, who drink in the scandalous gossip while maintaining their own strict social hierarchy. Robert Altmans period drama, starring Maggie Smith, Helen Mirren, Kelly Macdonald, Michael Gambon, Clive Owen and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The teams are unleashed on the pet industry as they are tasked with designing a product and social media campaign to pitch to some of the UKs biggest retailers. With one candidate in the firing line, its a battle for a place in the final five

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: St Michaels Mount: After the Storm
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Anita Rani head to St Michaels Mount in Cornwall after Storm Goretti. Hurricane-force winds ripped through the island, leaving the tiny community of just 33 residents facing a huge clean-up. With Easter approaching, they join the race to repair the damage, restore the landscape and reopen this iconic landmark to visitors. Plus, Datshiane Navanayagam investigates the rise of youth homelessness in rural Britain

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Reality game show with John Hannah as narrator. Five adventurous teams are pushed to the limit, battling extreme climates and harsh terrain in a 12,000km race from Palermo, Sicily, across Europe and Asia to reach Hatgal in northern Mongolia. Best friends Jo and Kush from Liverpool; siblings Katie and Harrison from Manchester; Molly and her father Andrew; cousins Puja and Roshni and in-laws Mark and Margo take on the challenge

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa tries to stay positive in the face of her fears as she discovers she is pregnant again, while Dave overhears Amanda talking about her ex and wants to know more

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp meets a Glasgow childrens entertainer who is desperate for a good nights sleep. Adding a hot bath to an evening routine has been shown to help improve sleep, reduce the risk of heart disease and even burn a few extra calories

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Jacqui and Frankie from The Traitors
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph recruits The Traitors star Frankie Rowan-Plowden to oversee the auction of a property in Kent and theres a cottage in Cornwall that has taken quirky to a new level. Martin Roberts is in Bristol to see a mid-terraced house

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fields of Danger as Farmer Is Impaled and Eagles Threaten Highland Flocks
programme.desc: Documentary following the police, fire and ambulance services as they attend emergency situations in rural communities. The East Anglian Air Ambulance races to save a farmer after a freak accident, lambs face attack from birds of prey, and reporter Paul Colgan investigates illegal fly-tipping in Cheshire. Featuring accounts from first responders, community members and farmers at the centre of these emergencies

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: Helen Skelton presents as the team dives in Rydal Water in the Lake District in the hopes of finding a cherished ring lost by a 91-year-old paddleboarder

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clives Italian adventure continues with visits to Verona, Treviso, Lake Garda and the village of Limone, along the way learning how to make tiramisu and finding out how the balcony inspired by Shakespeares Romeo and Juliet now brings lovers from all over the world. He also meets up with an old friend and learns why Italians live some of the longest lives in Europe

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the survival techniques used by the animal kingdoms smallest creatures. North Americas Sonoran Desert is home to the grasshopper mouse, which is capable of disarming scorpions and centipedes twice its size, and displays a power and athleticism that enables it to avoid the attentions of rattlesnakes and tarantulas. In Africa, the elephant shrew, known as the sengi, employs speed to keep its distance from predators, following a meticulously constructed - and maintained - network of tiny racetracks in the savannah. The film concludes with Hidden Kingdoms Revealed, a 10-minute report on how the documentary was made. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team have a few surprises in store for the residents of Caernarfon in Gwynedd. Geoffrey Munn values an attractive Chinese-style art deco jade necklace and Alan Middleton discovers a stunning 17th-century lantern clock. Meanwhile, the owner of a 200-year-old bleeding bowl bought for 25p from a junk shop is dumbfounded to learn its true value

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and David Fletcher travel to Warminster, Wiltshire, where they offer their opinions on peoples antiques and collectibles before they go to auction. Paul Martin also investigates the UFO sightings reported in the town in the 1960s and 70s

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy test their general knowledge skills in the second round of trivia-based games

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan continues his journey in Kerry, where he joins his brother for a fishing trip on Lough Leane, and fulfills some sporting dreams on the hurling pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East England: Starter and Fish
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for London and south-east England with canapes, starters and fish dishes. They are judged by veteran judge, Michelin-starred chef and former banquet fish course winner Aktar Islam. Their dishes include a Paddington-inspired take on savoury marmalade and a stuffed poached brill with a Vesper martini broth in a nod to Casino Royale

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth sets out on its first transatlantic crossing, a four-month deployment on the high seas which has the primary purpose of putting the top secret F-35B Lightning Stealth Fighter to the test. Within hours of leaving Portsmouth, the vessel encounters a Russian warship and needs to take immediate evasive action, and the crew has to deal with floods and dangerous fuel leaks on their way to Florida

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims are in the Scottish Borders on St Cuthberts Way in the Cheviots. It takes them on a strenuous route up Wideopen Hill to reach some spectacular views. Later, they head to Cuthberts Cave, in the Kelso Hills, and on the last day of their pilgrimage they follow the 5km walk across the tidal sands of the Pilgrims Way. Finally, they arrive at Holy Island and reflect on their journey

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster to find Northern Irelands Greatest Garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing between Ivydene Garden near Comber, a tropical backyard over in Lisburn, and a rock garden in Ballynahinch

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Inside Britains National Parks
programme.desc: Pembrokeshire Coast National Park offers breathtaking views of rugged shorelines, sandy beaches, and offshore islands. It is home to numerous seabird colonies, including guillemots and the endangered puffin, and its many bays provide a haven for grey seals and their pups. Beyond the coast, a diverse landscape of marshes, with salt and freshwater meadows support a variety of rare mammals and birds

programme.title: Killing Sherlock: Lucy Worsley on the Case of Conan Doyle
programme.desc: Lucy looks at what happened after the author killed off Sherlock Holmes, exploring his desire to distance himself from the sleuth. From the delights of the ski slopes to the horrors of the Boer War, she reveals how far Conan Doyle went to make himself the hero of his own story - including taking on the role of detective himself in one of the most important legal cases of the 20th century

programme.title: Weather Watchers with Barra Best
programme.desc: The weather presenter and journalist examines how climate shapes everyones lives and looks at the activities that it enables people to participate in

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.sub-title: The Hunt for Arlene Frasers Killer - Part One
programme.desc: First of a two-part examination of the 1998 disappearance of Elgin mother-of-two Arlene Fraser, highlighting her familys unbreakable determination to uncover the truth. What began as a missing-person report rapidly escalated into one of Scotlands most complex and high-profile investigations, but no-one could have foreseen the labyrinth of twists that would follow. With contributions from detectives, media and family members including Arlenes sister Carol Gillies

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon and guests examine the candidates performances and analyse the task, the boardrooms and the firings. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Alex Murphy. Conor and Jock finally reunite after Jock makes a lucky escape from the Colombian prison and becomes a stowaway on a ship bound for Cork

programme.title: Harry Clark Goes to Rome
programme.desc: The Traitors star goes on a deeply personal journey from Slough to Rome and Vatican City in search of answers about belief, identity and what it means to be a good Catholic in the modern world. Along the way, hes challenged on his lapsed habits, engages in traditional pilgrim practices and raises questions about the role of faith in modern Britain

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball explains how it isnt necessary to stop eating chocolate to improve health - as long as its dark chocolate. She travels to Birmingham where she shows fitness fanatic Bilal how chocolate with at least 70 per cent cocoa could boost heart health, improve brain function and even reduce the risk of type 2 diabetes

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Celebrity special, featuring Carol Smillie, who is in for a shock when she sees the state of the property Martin Roberts shows her around in Scotland. Plus, a look back at a 2006 episode, when Martin saw a house in a stunning location in Wiltshire, and Jacqui Joseph was in Warwickshire, where outside space was at a premium

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East England: Starter and Fish
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for London and south-east England with canapes, starters and fish dishes. They are judged by veteran judge, Michelin-starred chef and former banquet fish course winner Aktar Islam. Their dishes include a Paddington-inspired take on savoury marmalade and a stuffed poached brill with a Vesper martini broth in a nod to Casino Royale

programme.title: Egypts Unexplained Files
programme.desc: New technology is employed to decode some of Egypts most enduring enigmas, to reveal the hidden truths that are rewriting human history. This episode explores the ancient Egyptian obsession with death and the afterlife, including the secrets behind Egyptians worship of the pyramid shape

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman and Ancient Horn Mystery
programme.desc: The legendary Ohio Grassman is apparently captured in footage of a tall, dark-coloured, hairy creature walking through woods. Plus, a look at the Chilean family photograph of a real UFO flying above the Andes Mountains and the notion that a 5,000-year-old horn discovered on a beach in England belongs to a prehistoric creature that still lives

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Men in Black Calls, Old Kentucky Aliens and Mysterious Eclipse
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts examine several seemingly impossible events, such as a family managing to survive a bolt of lightning ripping through their car during a Kansas storm, a bizarre creature spotted on a driveway camera and an image captured of an alien probe

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Falcon 9 Mystery, Demonic Wall Climber and Leningrad Alien Fetus
programme.desc: A strange swirling vortex with a white centre seen in the skies over Oklahoma. Plus, a video said to be sealed in Vatican archives that shows a real case of demonic possession

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores Plymouths history during the Elizabethan era, visiting the home of the citys most famous son, Sir Francis Drake, and uncovering his history of piracy and slave trading - all carried out with approval from the Queen. She also discovers the revolutionary shipbuilding techniques that helped defeat the Spanish Armada, while aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson highlights the physical characteristics that made Plymouth Harbour such an attractive base for pirates

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts visits Portsmouth to investigate the history of the Royal Navy, uncovering some uncomfortable truths behind the myth surrounding Lord Horatio Nelson and getting first-hand experience of what life would have been like in the heat of battle at Trafalgar. She also visits an archaeological dig on Burrow Island and a lantern show at the New Theatre Royal, and discovers the brutal punishments doled out for even minor offences by the Victorian penal system

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Wolverhampton, where a music box arrives for David Tupman to value, and letters from former prime ministers Edward Heath and Harold Macmillan land on Stewart Hofgartners table. David is also joined by Debbie Serpell and Karen Dalmeny, who haggle over their favourite items

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick and Corey dart out of the shop to check out a 1968 convertible. Chum wants a piece of an oreodont fossil, while Rick has quite an experience when a seller sends in a Jimi Hendrix poster. Later, a Harry and the Hendersons doll hits the counter

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly: Henrys Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk and Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: Victor tries to end his losing streak in Waxahachie, but faces some tough competition

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: Ricky is confronted with one of his biggest fears, and Victor gets more than he bargained for when he pursues a motorcycle

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori take a risk on a large covered item during an unusual auction. Will the reveal rev their profits or stall their cash flow? American documentary following former boxer Brandon Bernier and his wife Lori, as they buy storage units at auction

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: A locker unleashes a swarm of more than just merchandise, while Jesse throws down to win antiques. Documentary following former boxer Brandon Bernier and his wife Lori, as they buy storage units at auction

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Workmen in Canada film an enormous and enraged humanoid figure appearing to throw a tree trunk in the forests of Alberta. Hollywood ballistics expert Matt Kutcher tries to work out what kind of beast could throw such an enormous weight

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: A bizarre trend happened in Ireland in the 1980s when thousands of people started seeing religious statues move. Some residents of a village reported seeing a statue of the Virgin Mary move. Others believed the phenomenon was just an optical illusion or a prank

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Delving into the mysteries surrounding the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, investigating the theories that he remained alive, secretly, for some time afterwards

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: People reveal details from their past lives as celebrities, while experts investigate evidence that suggests death may not be the end of ones existence

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Broadhaven is a sleepy community in south-west Wales, but since the late 1970s it has made headlines for being the UFO capital of Europe. In 1977, schoolchildren spotted a cigar-shaped aircraft. The sighting transforms the quiet seaside town into a UFO hot spot

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: According to folklore, pots of gold and wishes are granted if a Leprechaun is caught. These mysterious little creatures are seemingly only found in Ireland. Alleged sightings of leprechauns and fairies are nothing new in Ireland. In fact, many claim they are part of everyday life on the Emerald Isle. Experts debate whether these Celtic creatures are fact or fiction

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: God moves in mysterious ways and sometimes appears in mysterious places. This episode lools at claims of mysterious religious sightings. Why do so many people see a vision of Jesus Christ in seemingly normal sights such as a tree stump or an antique cabinet?

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The patterns in fields, which appear overnight and without any warning. What are crop circles, why are they there and what could they possibly signify? Do they come from other worlds?

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The mystery surrounding a mass produced series of paintings of crying children, a popular artwork in many houses in the 1980s

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Investigating a strange creature seen in a wooded area of rural Massachusetts by three different people who all gave similar descriptions. Could the so-called Dover Demon be a never before discovered animal or could it have come from another civilisation?

programme.title: Worlds Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The discovery of a mysterious 500-year-old manuscript, with almost 300 pages full of handwritten notes and illustrations

programme.title: The God Code
programme.desc: Referencing his book God Code, antiquities expert Timothy P Smith looks into the existence of a code hidden in the worlds oldest complete text of the Hebrew Bible, which some believe to be the actual word of God

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori take a risk on a large covered item during an unusual auction. Will the reveal rev their profits or stall their cash flow? American documentary following former boxer Brandon Bernier and his wife Lori, as they buy storage units at auction

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: When the team deploys a cutting-edge new technology on Skinwalker Ranch to study the mysterious Bubble, it leads to an unsettling revelation

programme.title: Beyond Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: Andy and Paul return to Miller Ranch, site of several cattle mutilations and highway deaths, to poke the hornets nest at the bridge dubbed The Miller Triangle by the Skinwalker team. They soon find more evidence that the two sites are linked

programme.title: Egypts Unexplained Files
programme.desc: The truth behind the Ancient Egyptian superpower known today. It tells the story of the Great Pyramid at Giza, and how one discovery is rewriting what is now known about how the pyramids were built

programme.title: Bermuda Triangle: Into Cursed Waters
programme.desc: Two British passenger planes, almost identical, named the Star Tiger and the Star Ariel, vanished in the 1940s. The team targets an unidentified wreck that might be the Star Ariel, which vanished with 20 passengers and crew onboard

programme.title: In Search of Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos heads to Peru to investigate whether the strange symbols covering its desert floor are key to proving aliens once visited Earth

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: For more than four decades, award-winning journalist Linda Moulton Howe has covered the most mysterious phenomena on Earth. Could her investigations on everything from bizarre animal deaths to crop formations and alien abductions lead others toward a profound new reality?

programme.title: UFOs: The White House Files
programme.desc: From Truman to Trump, there has been a legacy of secrecy and controversy surrounding UFOs and the US presidency. This programme ascertains whether these powerful leaders could be hiding the truth from the public

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: When the Golds uncover a series of bogus pawns, their investigation leads to a trusted employee and a confession. Reality series about the running of a pawn shop

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les and Seth visit a customer with a garage full of vintage cars. Against his childrens wishes, Les offers big money for one of the owners golden oldies

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: With Les and Seth away at the Autorama car show - and Ashley in charge of the store - the employees go a little crazy. Documentary series following the daily running of a pawn shop

programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos contemplates whether aliens that once visited Earth deliberately left a series of clues that mankind was eventually meant to decipher.
